steps.title: Identify the grammatical context
steps.content: The blank contains a verb that coordinates with “won” (connected by “and”). The “when he joined the New York Knicks for the 1947-48 season” clause gives a reference point in the past. Serving in WWII (1941-45) occurred before this reference point.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content: To show that one past action was completed before another past moment, use past perfect tense (“had” + past participle). “Had already served” correctly indicates WWII service was complete before joining the Knicks. Simple past (A) and continuous (B) don't establish this temporal relationship, and present tense (C) is factually wrong. That matches choice D — D is correct.
